terms.definition: Training or evaluation that incorporates information unavailable at real prediction time, producing optimistically biased performance estimates.
A feature that directly or indirectly encodes the label or post-outcome information, letting the model cheat without learning generalizable signal.
Overlap or statistical coupling between training and held-out sets so test rows influence fitting or preprocessing.
Fitting imputers, scalers, encoders, selectors, or vocabularies on data that includes validation or test rows.
Using future observations or features computed with future data to predict past or present outcomes in time-ordered problems.
